mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, which compresses valuation multiples and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, it signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer demand, which forces corporate earnings down and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses above the 6.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer spending strength and construction sector activity, which jointly drive positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in more than 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, rebounds in the industrial production index reflect rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, which generate positive short-term equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Industrial production rebounds fail to predict positive returns in more than 50% of recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction during inflationary shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and wage income, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth decouples from equity performance, yielding negative forward returns in more than 55% of expansion months across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth falls below 0.5 percent, it indicates demand exhaustion and impending contraction, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where GDP growth drops below the 0.5 percent threshold during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a rising federal funds rate creates a stagflationary pressure regime where higher discount rates and eroded real margins jointly suppress equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary easing cycles, increases in housing starts signal improved credit conditions and consumer confidence, which translate into positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Housing starts fail to predict positive returns in more than 55% of easing periods, or the signal breaks down when mortgage rates exceed 7.0 percent.
